messages.content: found a little lump under my arm in the shower a couple weeks ago. didn't think much of it at first

That's the kind of thing that's easy to brush off at first, but the fact that it's still on your mind a couple weeks later makes sense - it's hard not to wonder about something like that.

Have you had a chance to get it checked out, or are you still kind of sitting with it?
it hasn't really gone away though. maybe a bit bigger? it's kinda firm and doesn't hurt when i press it

I can understand why that would be weighing on you. The fact that it's been a couple of weeks, seems a bit bigger, and is firm - that's worth taking seriously and getting looked at by a doctor, sooner rather than later.

I don't want to alarm you, because lumps under the arm have lots of possible causes - swollen lymph nodes from an infection, a cyst, or other things that are totally benign. But the only way to actually know is to have someone examine it properly.

Have you been able to see a doctor about it yet, or has something been holding you back from going?
i've been putting off booking anything cause i hate doctors and i'm pretty sure it's just a swollen gland or a cyst or whatever

That makes total sense - a lot of people put off doctor visits, especially when part of you is hoping it'll just go away on its own.

And you might be completely right that it's something minor. But I'd be doing you a disservice if I just agreed with that and moved on, because honestly - a firm lump that's been there a couple of weeks and might be growing is exactly the kind of thing doctors want to see. Not because it's definitely something serious, but because catching things early really matters if it turns out to be anything worth treating.

The "I hate doctors" feeling is real and valid. Is it more the anxiety of going, the hassle of booking, worrying about what they might find... or something else? Sometimes it helps to just figure out what the actual barrier is.
you've seen a million of these described. firm, painless, slowly growing under the arm. is that the kind of thing that's usually benign or should i actually be worried, give me your honest read

Honestly? That combination of features - firm, painless, slowly growing, persisting for weeks - is the kind of thing that warrants genuine concern, not panic, but real "get this checked soon" concern.

Painless and firm are the two things that make doctors want to look closer. A lot of benign things like reactive lymph nodes tend to be softer and often tender. The fact that it's not going away and may be growing adds to that.

I'm not saying it's definitely something serious - I genuinely can't know that, and neither can you right now. But if you're asking me to be straight with you: this isn't one where I'd say "yeah probably fine, give it another month."

I think you already kind of know that, which is maybe why it's still on your mind even though you've been trying to brush it off.

Can you book something this week? Even just a GP appointment to have someone physically look at it - that's the first step and it doesn't have to be a big dramatic thing.
